goal: Port structural.types.hidden_mutators (out_parameters) to Structure; rename to canonical

summary.title: Port structural.types.hidden_mutators to Structure; close structural ports
summary.what: Adds Language.hidden_mutators() per-grammar (Python / JS / TS / Go / C / C++ overrides). Adds Structure.hidden_mutators() + HiddenMutator/HiddenMutation frozen records + slop.structure._hidden_mutators helper. Renames rule file out_parameters.py → hidden_mutators.py, function run_out_parameters → run_hidden_mutators, test test_out_parameters.py → test_hidden_mutators.py. Drops legacy_v2_shim. Deletes slop/_structural/out_parameters.py (955 lines). slop.structure.rules.__init__ drops its legacy_v2_shim import — ZERO structural shims remain.
summary.why: Eighth and final structural shim retirement. Closes out the v2.0 substrate cutover for the structural rule suite. Per-grammar mutation detection (which had its own _scan_python / _scan_c / _scan_cpp / _scan_js_text / _scan_go_text walkers in the legacy kernel) collapses onto a single Language hook per grammar.
summary.user_goal: Port structural.types.hidden_mutators (out_parameters) to Structure; rename to canonical

decisions.chose: Per-grammar Language.hidden_mutators returns mutation events directly
decisions.rationale: Mutation detection is structurally different per language (attribute-call walks for Python, assignment-LHS walks for C/C++, builtin-call walks for Go, member-expression walks for JS/TS). Splitting into smaller vocabulary primitives would duplicate the walking logic; a single per-grammar method returning (param, method, line) triples is cleaner.

decisions.chose: Drop JS text-tier regex fallback in favour of AST walking
decisions.rationale: Legacy JS scanner was text-tier (regex on .push/.splice/.set lines) with no function-context resolution — emitted `name='<unknown>'`. The new walker uses tree-sitter's member_expression node and resolves the enclosing function from Structure.callables(), giving proper attribution.

decisions.chose: C++ adds non-const reference parameter detection
decisions.rationale: Legacy C++ scanner already covered references; the new walker preserves that. ``T& p`` mutated via ``p = X`` or ``p.field = X`` is the canonical C++ out-parameter shape.

By-reference parameter passing is Ruby's default — every object passed in is mutable, and the language has no const annotation. Flagging mutation would be a constant false positive. Same posture as the legacy kernel (Ruby never registered).

review_notes: Six grammars now declare hidden_mutators; Ruby and the four others (Rust/Java/C#/Julia) return empty by base default.
The substrate walker is more accurate than the legacy text-tier JS/Go scanners — proper function attribution instead of emitting `<unknown>` per match line.
C++ helper functions reuse C's pointer-mutation walker via cross-module import (`from .c import _c_walk_pointer_mutations`) to avoid duplicating the LHS-pattern logic.
Structural rule suite is now 100% substrate-native: every rule consumes Structure directly; no legacy_v2_shim references remain in slop.structure.rules.

architectural_claims: Per-grammar mutation walkers collapse into one Language classmethod per grammar; the substrate iterates structure.callables() once and aggregates.
Structural rule suite is fully substrate-native — slop.structure.rules.__init__ has zero legacy_v2_shim references.
JS / Go mutation detection moves from text-tier regex to AST walking, gaining proper function-context attribution and shedding the legacy `name='<unknown>'` shape.
behavioral_changes: run_hidden_mutators signature changes from (root, rc, sc) to (structure, rc, sc); function renamed from run_out_parameters.
Rule file path changes from slop/structure/rules/out_parameters.py to slop/structure/rules/hidden_mutators.py.
Test file path changes from tests/test_rules/test_out_parameters.py to tests/test_rules/test_hidden_mutators.py.
JS / Go findings now carry the enclosing function name (legacy emitted `<unknown>`).
Summary keys functions_analyzed and files_searched are removed; callables_analyzed replaces them.
migration_notes: Custom code importing run_out_parameters from slop.structure.rules.out_parameters will break. Replacement is slop.structure.rules.hidden_mutators.run_hidden_mutators.
Custom code calling out_parameters_kernel directly will break (kernel deleted). Use Tree + Structure.hidden_mutators() instead.
